sections.title: A: Error correction
sections.instructions: Read the text. Some lines contain one extra word that should not be there. Click the extra word. If a line is correct, leave it as it is.

items.sentence: Last week, I entered a [few] local radio station competition asking if
they were giving away [the] free passes to a recording session. Two days later,
they emailed [to] me two tickets to attend a live broadcast. I went along with
my friend Carlos last Friday. It was [an] amazing! The programme
is called Spin the Wheel and listeners phone in to try to
win prizes by answering [a] lots of questions about music and culture,
like song titles, artists and album names. There are five rounds
in total and contestants get eliminated one by one as the show
goes on. I'd love to go [into] on a competition like that myself
but, for now, I'm [much] happy just to watch from the audience.

sections.title: B: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals
sections.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals when this is necessary.

items.sentence: Why can't (POLITICS) ...... ever give clear answers about policy issues?
I hoped this documentary would help my project, but it wasn't very (INFORM) .......
My older brother has kept a personal (JOURNAL) ...... since he was ten years old.
Did you hear that (ANNOUNCE) ...... on the radio about the road closure?
There seems to be an (WRITE) ...... rule that celebrities must always smile for the cameras.
I stood there in complete (BELIEVE) ...... when I heard my name called out on the show.
Online platforms have become a powerful means of (COMMUNICATE) .......
The presenter isn't exactly funny, but some of his comments are mildly (HUMOUR) .......

sections.title: C: Complete the second sentence using the word given
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

items.sentence:
These two candidates are very similar. **little**
There ...... these two candidates.

Her drama teacher greatly influenced her. **influence**
Her drama teacher ...... her.

She described the accident to the police reporter. **gave**
She ...... the accident to the police reporter.

A media group has just bought the TV channel. **control**
The TV channel is now ...... the media group.

You'll be wasting your time if you try to apply for that internship. **point**
There ...... to apply for that internship.

I believe that social media shapes public opinion. **view**
It ...... that social media shapes public opinion.

The editor will probably make an announcement tomorrow morning. **likely**
The editor ...... an announcement tomorrow morning.

A documentary about climate change will be shown instead of the film tonight. **place**
Tonight, the film, ...... a documentary about climate change will be shown.

The politician wouldn't say anything about the investigation. **comment**
The politician refused ...... the investigation.
How ...... coverage has this story received so far?
Only a ...... people manage to build a career in investigative journalism.
This news website has ...... of pop-up adverts on every page.
She earned ...... money by selling her photos to a national newspaper.
There's ...... chance of getting noticed without any social media presence.
I think that ...... news programmes these days have some kind of entertainment angle.
There aren't ...... opportunities for young journalists to find well-paid work.
The news bulletin usually ...... on just before nine.
I spotted the headline while I was ...... through a newspaper on the train.
Why did you have to bring that story ...... during the broadcast?
The reporter touched on the issue but didn't ...... into detail.
The gossip columnist completely ...... that story about the actor. It was totally false!
A promoter was ...... out complimentary copies of the new magazine to commuters.
The signal was poor, so I couldn't make ...... what the reporter was saying.
